description: A personal finance management app that lets users track income and expenses against user-defined budget categories, visualized through interactive charts. It provides actionable spending insights by comparing actual transactions against monthly budget targets, helping users identify overspending patterns over time.
features: Create and manage custom budget categories with monthly spending limits
Manual transaction entry with amount, date, category, and notes
CSV/bank statement import for bulk transaction entry
Dashboard with spending-vs-budget bar charts per category
Monthly trend line charts showing spending over 3/6/12 months
Pie chart breakdown of spending by category for any date range
Budget rollover: unspent amounts carry to next month
Transaction search and filter by date range, category, amount
Monthly summary email/notification when approaching budget limits
Multi-currency support with manual exchange rates
Savings goals tracker linked to specific categories
mvpFeatures: Create budget categories with monthly spending limits
Manual transaction entry (amount, date, category, note, income vs expense)
Dashboard with per-category spending-vs-budget progress bars and a pie chart
Monthly transaction list with filter by category and date range
Running monthly totals: total income, total expenses, net balance
userFlows: User registers → sets up first budget categories with limits → adds first transaction → lands on dashboard showing budget utilization
User adds transaction → selects category → category progress bar updates immediately → if over 90% of budget, inline warning is shown
User imports CSV → app parses rows → presents preview with auto-suggested category matches → user corrects mismatches → confirms import → transactions saved

User tries to delete a budget category that has existing transactions → warned that N transactions exist → prompted to reassign to another category or delete all associated transactions → confirms → category removed
User views monthly chart → clicks a bar segment → drills down into transaction list filtered to that category and month → edits a transaction inline → chart updates reactively
constraints: Dashboard must load with all charts rendered in under 2 seconds for up to 5 years of transaction history (~3,600 transactions)
Chart data aggregation queries must complete under 300ms p95
Transaction entry completable in under 4 user interactions (clicks/taps)
CSV import must handle files up to 10,000 rows without timeout
All monetary values stored as integers (cents) to avoid floating-point rounding errors
Date handling must be timezone-aware; budget periods based on user's local timezone
risks: Chart aggregation performance degrades as transaction volume grows — pre-computing MonthlyBudgetSnapshots at write time vs. querying raw transactions at read time is an architectural decision needed upfront
CSV import format varies wildly between banks — building a flexible parser vs. supporting named bank presets are two very different scopes; a naive parser will fail real-world files
Budget period definition (calendar month vs. rolling 30 days vs. custom pay-period) affects nearly every query and display logic — defaulting to calendar month may not match user payroll cycles
Floating-point currency arithmetic is a silent data corruption risk — integer (cents) storage must be enforced at the ORM/validation layer, not just convention
openQuestions: Is this single-user only or will households need shared access to the same budget? — multi-user sharing requires a Workspace/Household entity layer and permission model

Should budget limits reset every calendar month automatically, or should users define custom budget periods (e.g., biweekly paychecks)? — this changes the MonthlyBudgetSnapshot schema and all aggregation logic
Will bank/account syncing (Plaid, Open Banking) be in scope eventually? — if yes, the Transaction entity needs an external_id and sync_source field from day one to prevent duplicate imports later
